```{r}
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-ease-trial-expl.pdf",width=7,height=4)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-ease-trial-expl.png",width=7,height=4)

print(plot.ease.trial.expl)
```

### sharp / dull
#### response by prev_talker
```{r}
plot.sharp.resp.expl <- df.sharp2b %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = talker, 
                       y = response_sharp,
                       fill = talker)) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean",
               geom = "bar") + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot",
               geom = "linerange") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,1)) +
  labs(x = "Talker",
       y = "Mean Proportion 'Sharp'",
       title = "Mean Sharp/Dull Responses by Talker") + 
  facet_grid(~prev_talker,
             labeller = labeller(prev_talker = prev_talker.labs)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cbPalette,
                    name = "Talker")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/2b-plot-sharp-resp-expl.pdf",width=7,height=4)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/2b-plot-sharp-resp-expl.png",width=7,height=4)

print(plot.sharp.resp.expl)
```

#### response by trial
```{r}
# trend within block + indiv obs
plot.sharp.trial.expl <- df.sharp %>% 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = trial_in_block_recode, 
                       y = response_sharp, 
                       group = talker,
                       color = talker)) + 
  geom_smooth(se = T) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,1)) +
  facet_grid(study~block_rel) +
  labs(x = "Within-Block Trial #",
       y = "Mean Proportion 'Sharp'",
       title = "Mean Sharp/Dull Responses by Talker and Trial") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-sharp-trial-expl.pdf",width=7,height=4)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-sharp-trial-expl.png",width=7,height=4)

print(plot.sharp.trial.expl)
```

### emotive / robotic
#### response by prev_talker
```{r}
plot.express.resp.expl <- df.express2b %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = talker, 
                       y = response_emo,
                       fill = talker)) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean",
               geom = "bar") + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot",
               geom = "linerange") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,1)) +
  labs(x = "Talker",
       y = "Mean Proportion 'Emotive'",
       title = "Mean Emotive/Robotic Responses by Talker") + 
  facet_grid(~prev_talker,
             labeller = labeller(prev_talker = prev_talker.labs)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cbPalette,
                    name = "Talker")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/2b-plot-express-resp-expl.pdf",width=7,height=4)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/2b-plot-express-resp-expl.png",width=7,height=4)

print(plot.express.resp.expl)
```

#### response by trial
```{r}
# trend within block + indiv obs
plot.express.trial.expl <- df.express %>% 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = trial_in_block_recode, 
                       y = response_emo, 
                       group = talker,
                       color = talker)) + 
  geom_smooth(se = T) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,1)) +
  facet_grid(study~block_rel) +
  labs(x = "Within-Block Trial #",
       y = "Mean Proportion 'Emotive'",
       title = "Mean Emotive/Robotic Responses by Talker and Trial") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-express-trial-expl.pdf",width=7,height=4)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-express-trial-expl.png",width=7,height=4)

print(plot.express.trial.expl)
```

### extremity stuff
```{r}
# Calculate extremity (distance from chance)
df.extremes.gender <- df.gender %>%
  group_by(study, subject_id, talker) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    proportion = mean(response_fem), # assuming binary 0/1
    extremity = abs(proportion - 0.5),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df.extremes.ease <- df.ease %>%
  group_by(study, subject_id, talker) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    proportion = mean(response_com), # assuming binary 0/1
    extremity = abs(proportion - 0.5),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df.extremes.sharp <- df.sharp %>%
  group_by(study, subject_id, talker) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    proportion = mean(response_sharp), # assuming binary 0/1
    extremity = abs(proportion - 0.5),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

df.extremes.express <- df.express %>%
  group_by(study, subject_id, talker) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    proportion = mean(response_emo), # assuming binary 0/1
    extremity = abs(proportion - 0.5),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Correlations by response pair
correlations.gender <- df.extremes.gender %>%
  group_by(study) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(extremity, mean_rt, use = "complete.obs"),
    p = cor.test(extremity, mean_rt)$p.value,
    n = n()
  )

correlations.ease <- df.extremes.ease %>%
  group_by(study) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(extremity, mean_rt, use = "complete.obs"),
    p = cor.test(extremity, mean_rt)$p.value,
    n = n()
  )

correlations.sharp <- df.extremes.sharp %>%
  group_by(study) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(extremity, mean_rt, use = "complete.obs"),
    p = cor.test(extremity, mean_rt)$p.value,
    n = n()
  )

correlations.express <- df.extremes.express %>%
  group_by(study) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(extremity, mean_rt, use = "complete.obs"),
    p = cor.test(extremity, mean_rt)$p.value,
    n = n()
  )

print(correlations.gender)
print(correlations.ease)
print(correlations.sharp)
print(correlations.express)
```

```{r}
plot.gender.extreme <- ggplot(data = filter(df.extremes.gender,
                                             talker == "1F" | talker == "2F"), 
                              mapping = aes(x = extremity, 
                                            y = mean_rt, 
                                            color = talker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.01)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              color = "black") + 
  facet_grid(~study) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(300, 3500)) + 
  labs(x = "Extremity (Distance from Average At-Chance 'Feminine' Response)",
       y = "Mean Response Time from Stimulus Onset (ms)",
       title = "Mean Response Times Predicted by Extremity") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")

plot.ease.extreme <- ggplot(data = filter(df.extremes.ease,
                                             talker == "1F" | talker == "2F"), 
                              mapping = aes(x = extremity, 
                                            y = mean_rt, 
                                            color = talker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.01)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              color = "black") + 
  facet_grid(~study) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(300, 3500)) +
  labs(x = "Extremity (Distance from Average At-Chance 'Comfortable' Response)",
       y = "Mean Response Time from Stimulus Onset (ms)",
       title = "Mean Response Times Predicted by Extremity") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")

plot.sharp.extreme <- ggplot(data = filter(df.extremes.sharp,
                                             talker == "1F" | talker == "2F"), 
                              mapping = aes(x = extremity, 
                                            y = mean_rt, 
                                            color = talker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.01)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              color = "black") + 
  facet_grid(~study) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(300, 3500)) +
  labs(x = "Extremity (Distance from Average At-Chance 'Sharp' Response)",
       y = "Mean Response Time from Stimulus Onset (ms)",
       title = "Mean Response Times Predicted by Extremity") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")

plot.express.extreme <- ggplot(data = filter(df.extremes.express,
                                             talker == "1F" | talker == "2F"),
                              mapping = aes(x = extremity, 
                                            y = mean_rt, 
                                            color = talker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.01)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              color = "black") + 
  facet_grid(~study) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(300, 3500)) +
  labs(x = "Extremity (Distance from Average At-Chance 'Emotive' Response)",
       y = "Mean Response Time from Stimulus Onset (ms)",
       title = "Mean Response Times Predicted by Extremity") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cbPalette2,
                    name = "Talker")
  
```


```{r}
(plot.gender.extreme + plot.ease.extreme) /
(plot.sharp.extreme + plot.express.extreme) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    tag_prefix = "",
    tag_suffix = "",
    theme = theme(
      plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    )
  ) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 4),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 5),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 0),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-extreme.pdf",width=9,height=5)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-plot-extreme.png",width=9,height=5)
```

### response variability over trials 
```{r}
# Step 1: Identify first and last quartile of trials within each block
df.gender.position <- df.gender %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel) %>%
  arrange(trial_in_block_recode) %>%  # Make sure trials are in order
  mutate(
    trial_number_in_block = row_number(),
    total_trials_in_block = n(),
    # Determine if trial is in first or last quartile
    position = case_when(
      trial_number_in_block <= ceiling(total_trials_in_block * 0.25) ~ "first_quart",
      trial_number_in_block > floor(total_trials_in_block * 0.75) ~ "last_quart",
      TRUE ~ "middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Keep only first and last quartile
  filter(position %in% c("first_quart", "last_quart"))

df.gender.part.var <- df.gender.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_within = mean(response_fem),
    sd_within = sd(response_fem),
    cv_within = (sd(response_fem, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(response_fem, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) 
df.gender.part.ag <- df.gender.part.var %>%
  group_by(talker, study, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_mean_within = mean(mean_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_within_sd = mean(sd_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Step 2: Calculate mean proportion for each participant-talker-study-response_type-quartile combination
df.gender.pos.ag <- df.gender.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    proportion = mean(response_fem, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Step 3: Calculate variability (SD or variance) for each talker-study-response_type-quartile combination
df.gender.var <- df.gender.pos.ag %>%
  group_by(talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_proportion = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_proportion = sd(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance = var(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_participants = n(),
    se = sd_proportion / sqrt(n_participants),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Step 6: Visualize variability - Option 3: Violin plots with points
library(ggdist)

df.gender.pos.ag %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = position, y = proportion, fill = position)) +
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 1,
    width = 0.6,
    .width = 0,
    justification = -0.2,
    point_colour = NA
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.12,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.05, height = 0, seed = 123),
    alpha = 0.3,
    size = 1
  ) +
  facet_grid(study ~ talker) +
  labs(
    x = "Block Position",
    y = "Proportion",
    title = "Response Variability: First vs Last Quartile"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )

# Step 7: Visualize variability - Option 4: Line plot showing change in variability
ggplot(df.gender.var, 
       aes(x = position, y = sd_proportion, 
           color = talker, group = talker)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_grid(study ~ block_rel) +
  labs(
    x = "Block Position",
    y = "Standard Deviation",
    color = "Talker",
    title = "Change in Variability from First to Last Quartile"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
# Step 1: Calculate within-participant variability
df.gender.position <- df.gender %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel) %>%
  arrange(trial_in_block_recode) %>%  # Make sure trials are in order
  mutate(
    trial_number_in_block = row_number(),
    total_trials_in_block = n(),
    # Determine if trial is in first or last quartile
    position = case_when(
      trial_number_in_block <= ceiling(total_trials_in_block * 0.25) ~ "first_quart",
      trial_number_in_block > floor(total_trials_in_block * 0.75) ~ "last_quart",
      TRUE ~ "middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Keep only first and last quartile
  filter(position %in% c("first_quart", "last_quart"))

df.gender.part.var <- df.gender.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_response = mean(response_fem),
    sd_response = sd(response_fem),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) 

# Step 2: Calculate group-level means and SDs
df.gender.part.sum <- df.gender.part.var %>%
  group_by(talker, study, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_of_means = mean(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_of_sds = mean(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_of_means = sd(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    se_of_sds = sd(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

plot.gender.mean.expl <- ggplot(df.gender.part.sum, 
                 aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_means, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Talker", y = "Mean Response", fill = "Quartile") +
  theme_minimal()

plot.gender.sd.expl <- ggplot(df.gender.part.sum, 
               aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_sds, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,0.35)) +
  labs(x = "Talker", 
       y = "Response Variability (SD)", 
       fill = "Quartile", 
       title = "Feminine/Masculine Trials") 

plot.gender.mean.expl / plot.gender.sd.expl + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Calculate within-participant variability
df.ease.position <- df.ease %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel) %>%
  arrange(trial_in_block_recode) %>%  # Make sure trials are in order
  mutate(
    trial_number_in_block = row_number(),
    total_trials_in_block = n(),
    # Determine if trial is in first or last quartile
    position = case_when(
      trial_number_in_block <= ceiling(total_trials_in_block * 0.25) ~ "first_quart",
      trial_number_in_block > floor(total_trials_in_block * 0.75) ~ "last_quart",
      TRUE ~ "middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Keep only first and last quartile
  filter(position %in% c("first_quart", "last_quart"))

df.ease.part.var <- df.ease.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_response = mean(response_com),
    sd_response = sd(response_com),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) 

# Step 2: Calculate group-level means and SDs
df.ease.part.sum <- df.ease.part.var %>%
  group_by(talker, study, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_of_means = mean(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_of_sds = mean(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_of_means = sd(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    se_of_sds = sd(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

plot.ease.mean.expl <- ggplot(df.ease.part.sum, 
                 aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_means, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Talker", y = "Mean Response", fill = "Quartile") +
  theme_minimal()

plot.ease.sd.expl <- ggplot(df.ease.part.sum, 
               aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_sds, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,0.35)) +
  labs(x = "Talker", 
       y = "Variability (SD)", 
       fill = "Quartile", 
       title = "Comfortable/Awkward Trials") 

plot.ease.mean.expl / plot.ease.sd.expl + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Calculate within-participant variability
df.sharp.position <- df.sharp %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel) %>%
  arrange(trial_in_block_recode) %>%  # Make sure trials are in order
  mutate(
    trial_number_in_block = row_number(),
    total_trials_in_block = n(),
    # Determine if trial is in first or last quartile
    position = case_when(
      trial_number_in_block <= ceiling(total_trials_in_block * 0.25) ~ "first_quart",
      trial_number_in_block > floor(total_trials_in_block * 0.75) ~ "last_quart",
      TRUE ~ "middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Keep only first and last quartile
  filter(position %in% c("first_quart", "last_quart"))

df.sharp.part.var <- df.sharp.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_response = mean(response_sharp),
    sd_response = sd(response_sharp),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) 

# Step 2: Calculate group-level means and SDs
df.sharp.part.sum <- df.sharp.part.var %>%
  group_by(talker, study, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_of_means = mean(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_of_sds = mean(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_of_means = sd(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    se_of_sds = sd(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

plot.sharp.mean.expl <- ggplot(df.sharp.part.sum, 
                 aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_means, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Talker", y = "Mean Response", fill = "Quartile") +
  theme_minimal()

plot.sharp.sd.expl <- ggplot(df.sharp.part.sum, 
               aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_sds, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,0.35)) +
  labs(x = "Talker", 
       y = "Response Variability (SD)", 
       fill = "Quartile",
       title = "Sharp/Dull Trials") 

plot.sharp.mean.expl / plot.sharp.sd.expl + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

```{r}
# Step 1: Calculate within-participant variability
df.express.position <- df.express %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel) %>%
  arrange(trial_in_block_recode) %>%  # Make sure trials are in order
  mutate(
    trial_number_in_block = row_number(),
    total_trials_in_block = n(),
    # Determine if trial is in first or last quartile
    position = case_when(
      trial_number_in_block <= ceiling(total_trials_in_block * 0.25) ~ "first_quart",
      trial_number_in_block > floor(total_trials_in_block * 0.75) ~ "last_quart",
      TRUE ~ "middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Keep only first and last quartile
  filter(position %in% c("first_quart", "last_quart"))

df.express.part.var <- df.express.position %>%
  group_by(subject_id, talker, study, block_rel, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_response = mean(response_emo),
    sd_response = sd(response_emo),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) 

# Step 2: Calculate group-level means and SDs
df.express.part.sum <- df.express.part.var %>%
  group_by(talker, study, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_of_means = mean(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_of_sds = mean(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_of_means = sd(mean_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    se_of_sds = sd(sd_response, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

plot.express.mean.expl <- ggplot(df.express.part.sum, 
                 aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_means, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Talker", y = "Mean Response", fill = "Quartile") 

plot.express.sd.expl <- ggplot(df.express.part.sum, 
               aes(x = talker, y = mean_of_sds, fill = position)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(~study,
             scales = "free_x") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,0.35)) +
  labs(x = "Talker", 
       y = "Response Variability (SD)", 
       fill = "Quartile",
       title = "Emotive/Robotic Trials")

plot.express.mean.expl / plot.express.sd.expl + plot_layout(guides = "collect")
```

```{r}
(plot.gender.sd.expl + plot.ease.sd.expl) /
(plot.sharp.sd.expl + plot.express.sd.expl) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    tag_prefix = "",
    tag_suffix = "",
    theme = theme(
      plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    )
  ) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 6),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-sd-expl.pdf",width=8,height=5)
ggsave(file="./analysis/graphs/joint-sd-expl.png",width=8,height=5)
```
